### Install Tailwind CSS and initialize configuration

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/angular

Install Tailwind CSS, PostCSS, and Autoprefixer via npm, then generate the `tailwind.config.js` file.

```Terminal
npm install -D tailwindcss@3 postcss autoprefixernpx tailwindcss init
```

--------------------------------

### Start the React development server

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/create-react-app

Run your build process with `npm run start` to launch the Create React App development server and see your changes.

```bash
npm run start
```

--------------------------------

### Start Phoenix development server

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/phoenix

Run the Phoenix server to start the development environment with Tailwind watchers enabled.

```bash
mix phx.server
```

--------------------------------

### Install Tailwind CSS and dependencies

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/sveltekit

Install tailwindcss, postcss, and autoprefixer, then generate the configuration files.

```terminal
npm install -D tailwindcss@3 postcss autoprefixer
npx tailwindcss init -p
```

--------------------------------

### Install Tailwind CSS and dependencies

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/adonisjs

Install `tailwindcss`, `postcss`, and `autoprefixer` as development dependencies, then initialize Tailwind CSS.

```bash
npm install -D tailwindcss@3 postcss autoprefixernpx tailwindcss init -p
```

--------------------------------

### Start the development build process

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/adonisjs

Run the development server to compile assets and apply Tailwind CSS.

```bash
npm run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install Tailwind CSS and initialize config

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/create-react-app

Install tailwindcss as a development dependency via npm, and then run the init command to generate your tailwind.config.js file.

```bash
npm install -D tailwindcss@3npx tailwindcss init
```

--------------------------------

### Start the build process

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/symfony

Run the development build process to watch for file changes and recompile assets.

```terminal
npm run watch
```

--------------------------------

### Install postcss-import

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/using-with-preprocessors

Install the `postcss-import` plugin as a development dependency using npm. This plugin handles `@import` statements at build time.

```bash
npm install -D postcss-import
```

--------------------------------

### Install Tailwind CSS and dependencies

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/gatsby

Install Tailwind CSS v3, PostCSS, autoprefixer, and the Gatsby PostCSS plugin, then generate configuration files.

```bash
npm install -D tailwindcss@3 postcss autoprefixer gatsby-plugin-postcssnpx tailwindcss init -p
```

--------------------------------

### Install Tailwind CSS Typography Plugin

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/typography-plugin

Install the `@tailwindcss/typography` plugin using npm as a development dependency.

```bash
npm install -D @tailwindcss/typography
```

--------------------------------

### Install Tailwind CSS and dependencies

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/remix

Install tailwindcss, postcss, and autoprefixer as dev dependencies, then generate the configuration files tailwind.config.ts and postcss.config.js.

```bash
npm install -D tailwindcss@3 postcss autoprefixernpx tailwindcss init --ts -p
```

--------------------------------

### Install Tailwind CSS and PostCSS

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/parcel

Install Tailwind CSS v3 and PostCSS as dev dependencies, then generate the default tailwind.config.js configuration file.

```bash
npm install -D tailwindcss@3 postcssnpx tailwindcss init
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new Vite React project

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/vite

Initializes a new React project using Vite, creating a project directory and installing initial dependencies.

```bash
npm create vite@latest my-project -- --template reactcd my-project
```

--------------------------------

### Install Tailwind CSS and PostCSS dependencies

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/emberjs

Install Tailwind CSS, PostCSS, postcss-loader, and autoprefixer as development dependencies, then initialize Tailwind CSS to generate its configuration files.

```bash
npm install -D tailwindcss@3 postcss postcss-loader autoprefixernpx tailwindcss init -p
```

--------------------------------

### Build Responsive Layout Components

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/responsive-design

Example of a marketing component that uses a stacked layout on small screens and a flex layout on medium screens.

```html
<div class="max-w-md mx-auto bg-white rounded-xl shadow-md overflow-hidden md:max-w-2xl">
  <div class="md:flex">
    <div class="md:shrink-0">
      <img class="h-48 w-full object-cover md:h-full md:w-48" src="/img/building.jpg" alt="Modern building architecture">
    </div>
    <div class="p-8">
      <div class="uppercase tracking-wide text-sm text-indigo-500 font-semibold">Company retreats</div>
      <a href="#" class="block mt-1 text-lg leading-tight font-medium text-black hover:underline">Incredible accommodation for your team</a>
      <p class="mt-2 text-slate-500">Looking to take your team away on a retreat to enjoy awesome food and take in some sunshine? We have a list of places to do just that.</p>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

--------------------------------

### Start the Tailwind CSS build process

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/ruby-on-rails

Run the development server to compile your Tailwind CSS and watch for changes.

```bash
./bin/dev
```

--------------------------------

### Start Parcel build process

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/parcel

Run the Parcel development server to build and serve your project with hot module reloading.

```bash
npx parcel src/index.html
```

--------------------------------

### Install Autoprefixer via npm

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/browser-support

Installs Autoprefixer as a development dependency, which automatically adds vendor prefixes to CSS.

```bash
npm install -D autoprefixer
```

--------------------------------

### Start Gatsby development server

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/gatsby

Run the Gatsby development server to build and serve your project with Tailwind CSS.

```bash
gatsby develop
```

--------------------------------

### Install Tailwind CLI

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/phoenix

Download the standalone Tailwind CLI and generate the tailwind.config.js file in the assets directory.

```bash
mix tailwind.install
```

--------------------------------

### Generated CSS with prefix and important

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/plugins

Example of how static utilities are transformed when prefix and important settings are active.

```css
.tw-content-auto {
  content-visibility: auto !important;
}
.tw-content-hidden {
  content-visibility: hidden !important;
}
.tw-content-visible {
  content-visibility: visible !important;
}
```

--------------------------------

### Start Angular development server

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/angular

Run the Angular development server to build and serve your application with Tailwind CSS.

```Terminal
ng serve
```

--------------------------------

### Apply snap-start with Tailwind CSS

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/scroll-snap-align

Use the `snap-start` utility to align an element to the start of its scroll container when scrolling.

```html
<div class="snap-x ...">
  <div class="snap-start ...">
    <img src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1604999565976-8913ad2ddb7c?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=320&h=160&q=80" />
  </div>
  <div class="snap-start ...">
    <img src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1540206351-d6465b3ac5c1?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=320&h=160&q=80" />
  </div>
  <div class="snap-start ...">
    <img src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1622890806166-111d7f6c7c97?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=320&h=160&q=80" />
  </div>
  <div class="snap-start ...">
    <img src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1590523277543-a94d2e4eb00b?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=320&h=160&q=80" />
  </div>
  <div class="snap-start ...">
    <img src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1575424909138-46b05e5919ec?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=320&h=160&q=80" />
  </div>
  <div class="snap-start ...">
    <img src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1559333086-b0a56225a93c?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=MnwxMjA3fDB8MHxwaG90by1wYWdlfHx8fGVufDB8fHx8&auto=format&fit=crop&w=320&h=160&q=80" />
  </div>
</div>
```

--------------------------------

### Start the Tailwind CLI build process

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/installation

Run the CLI to watch for changes in template files and compile the final CSS output.

```terminal
npx tailwindcss -i ./src/input.css -o ./src/output.css --watch
```

--------------------------------

### Basic alignment and distribution with place-content

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/place-content

Use these utilities to control the alignment of grid items. These examples demonstrate centering, start/end alignment, and various spacing distributions.

```html
<div class="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4 place-content-center h-48 ...">
  <div>01</div>
  <div>02</div>
  <div>03</div>
  <div>04</div>
</div>
```

```html
<div class="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4 place-content-start h-48 ...">
  <div>01</div>
  <div>02</div>
  <div>03</div>
  <div>04</div>
</div>
```

```html
<div class="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4 place-content-end h-48 ...">
  <div>01</div>
  <div>02</div>
  <div>03</div>
  <div>04</div>
</div>
```

```html
<div class="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4 place-content-between h-48 ...">
  <div>01</div>
  <div>02</div>
  <div>03</div>
  <div>04</div>
</div>
```

```html
<div class="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4 place-content-around h-48 ...">
  <div>01</div>
  <div>02</div>
  <div>03</div>
  <div>04</div>
</div>
```

```html
<div class="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4 place-content-evenly h-48 ...">
  <div>01</div>
  <div>02</div>
  <div>03</div>
  <div>04</div>
</div>
```

```html
<div class="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4 place-content-stretch h-48 ...">
  <div>01</div>
  <div>02</div>
  <div>03</div>
  <div>04</div>
</div>
```

--------------------------------

### Adding Drop Shadows to Elements

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/drop-shadow

Basic usage examples demonstrating how to apply drop shadow utilities to HTML elements using the drop-shadow-* classes.

```APIDOC
## Adding Drop Shadows

### Description
Apply drop shadow effects to elements using the drop-shadow-* utility classes. These classes add CSS drop-shadow filters that work well with irregular shapes.

### Usage

```html
<div class="drop-shadow-md">
  <!-- Element with medium drop shadow -->
</div>
<div class="drop-shadow-lg">
  <!-- Element with large drop shadow -->
</div>
<div class="drop-shadow-xl">
  <!-- Element with extra large drop shadow -->
</div>
<div class="drop-shadow-2xl">
  <!-- Element with 2x large drop shadow -->
</div>
```

### Notes
- Use drop-shadow utilities for irregular shapes like text and SVG elements
- For regular rectangular elements, consider using box-shadow instead
- Available sizes: sm, (default), md, lg, xl, 2xl, none
```

--------------------------------

### HTML Example with Tailwind Classes

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/content-configuration

Shows an HTML structure styled with various Tailwind utility classes, demonstrating how the framework scans for complete class strings.

```html
<div class="md:flex">
  <div class="md:flex-shrink-0">
    <img class="rounded-lg md:w-56" src="/img/shopping.jpg" alt="Woman paying for a purchase">
  </div>
  <div class="mt-4 md:mt-0 md:ml-6">
    <div class="uppercase tracking-wide text-sm text-indigo-600 font-bold">
      Marketing
    </div>
    <a href="/get-started" class="block mt-1 text-lg leading-tight font-semibold text-gray-900 hover:underline">
      Finding customers for your new business
    </a>
    <p class="mt-2 text-gray-600">
      Getting a new business off the ground is a lot of hard work.
      Here are five ideas you can use to find your first customers.
    </p>
  </div>
</div>
```

--------------------------------

### Align Content to Start with Tailwind CSS

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/align-content

Use `content-start` to pack rows against the beginning of the cross axis in a grid or flex container.

```html
<div class="h-56 grid grid-cols-3 gap-4 content-start ...">
  <div>01</div>
  <div>02</div>
  <div>03</div>
  <div>04</div>
  <div>05</div>
</div>
```

--------------------------------

### Install Webpack Encore

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/symfony

Add the Webpack Encore bundle to manage asset compilation in your Symfony project.

```terminal
composer require symfony/webpack-encore-bundle
```

--------------------------------

### Install Tailwind CSS in Astro

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/astro

Add Tailwind CSS and the Astro Tailwind integration to your project using the `astro add` command.

```bash
npx astro add tailwind
```

### Create a new Qwik project

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/qwik

Initialize a new project using the Qwik CLI.

```terminal
npm create qwik@latest my-projectcd my-project
```

--------------------------------

### Example of Repeated Utility Classes for Avatars

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/reusing-styles

This HTML snippet demonstrates a common scenario where Tailwind CSS utility classes are repeated for similar elements, such as avatar images. It highlights the problem of duplication that this guide aims to address.

```html
<div>
  <div class="flex items-center space-x-2 text-base">
    <h4 class="font-semibold text-slate-900">Contributors</h4>
    <span class="rounded-full bg-slate-100 px-2 py-1 text-xs font-semibold text-slate-700">204</span>
  </div>
  <div class="mt-3 flex -space-x-2 overflow-hidden">
    <img class="inline-block h-12 w-12 rounded-full ring-2 ring-white" src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1491528323818-fdd1faba62cc?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=eyJhcHBfaWQiOjEyMDd9&auto=format&fit=facearea&facepad=2&w=256&h=256&q=80" alt=""/>
    <img class="inline-block h-12 w-12 rounded-full ring-2 ring-white" src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1550525811-e5869dd03032?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&auto=format&fit=facearea&facepad=2&w=256&h=256&q=80" alt=""/>
    <img class="inline-block h-12 w-12 rounded-full ring-2 ring-white" src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1500648767791-00dcc994a43e?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=eyJhcHBfaWQiOjEyMDd9&auto=format&fit=facearea&facepad=2.25&w=256&h=256&q=80" alt=""/>
    <img class="inline-block h-12 w-12 rounded-full ring-2 ring-white" src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1472099645785-5658abf4ff4e?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=eyJhcHBfaWQiOjEyMDd9&auto=format&fit=facearea&facepad=2&w=256&h=256&q=80" alt=""/>
    <img class="inline-block h-12 w-12 rounded-full ring-2 ring-white" src="https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1517365830460-955ce3ccd263?ixlib=rb-1.2.1&ixid=eyJhcHBfaWQiOjEyMDd9&auto=format&fit=facearea&facepad=2&w=256&h=256&q=80" alt=""/>
  </div>
  <div class="mt-3 text-sm font-medium">
    <a href="#" class="text-blue-500">+ 198 others</a>
  </div>
</div>
```

--------------------------------

### Border Radius Utilities Reference

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/border-radius

Complete reference guide for Tailwind CSS border radius utility classes. Includes all-corner rounding, side-specific rounding (start, end, top, right, bottom), and size variants from none to full.

```APIDOC
# Tailwind CSS Border Radius Utilities

## General Border Radius Classes

### All Corners

| Class | CSS Property | Value |
|-------|--------------|-------|
| `rounded-none` | border-radius | 0px |
| `rounded-sm` | border-radius | 0.125rem (2px) |
| `rounded` | border-radius | 0.25rem (4px) |
| `rounded-md` | border-radius | 0.375rem (6px) |
| `rounded-lg` | border-radius | 0.5rem (8px) |
| `rounded-xl` | border-radius | 0.75rem (12px) |
| `rounded-2xl` | border-radius | 1rem (16px) |
| `rounded-3xl` | border-radius | 1.5rem (24px) |
| `rounded-full` | border-radius | 9999px |

## Side-Specific Border Radius Classes

### Start Side (rounded-s-*)

| Class | CSS Properties |
|-------|----------------|
| `rounded-s-none` | border-start-start-radius: 0px; border-end-start-radius: 0px; |
| `rounded-s-sm` | border-start-start-radius: 0.125rem (2px); border-end-start-radius: 0.125rem (2px); |
| `rounded-s` | border-start-start-radius: 0.25rem (4px); border-end-start-radius: 0.25rem (4px); |
| `rounded-s-md` | border-start-start-radius: 0.375rem (6px); border-end-start-radius: 0.375rem (6px); |
| `rounded-s-lg` | border-start-start-radius: 0.5rem (8px); border-end-start-radius: 0.5rem (8px); |
| `rounded-s-xl` | border-start-start-radius: 0.75rem (12px); border-end-start-radius: 0.75rem (12px); |
| `rounded-s-2xl` | border-start-start-radius: 1rem (16px); border-end-start-radius: 1rem (16px); |
| `rounded-s-3xl` | border-start-start-radius: 1.5rem (24px); border-end-start-radius: 1.5rem (24px); |
| `rounded-s-full` | border-start-start-radius: 9999px; border-end-start-radius: 9999px; |

### End Side (rounded-e-*)

| Class | CSS Properties |
|-------|----------------|
| `rounded-e-none` | border-start-end-radius: 0px; border-end-end-radius: 0px; |
| `rounded-e-sm` | border-start-end-radius: 0.125rem (2px); border-end-end-radius: 0.125rem (2px); |
| `rounded-e` | border-start-end-radius: 0.25rem (4px); border-end-end-radius: 0.25rem (4px); |
| `rounded-e-md` | border-start-end-radius: 0.375rem (6px); border-end-end-radius: 0.375rem (6px); |
| `rounded-e-lg` | border-start-end-radius: 0.5rem (8px); border-end-end-radius: 0.5rem (8px); |
| `rounded-e-xl` | border-start-end-radius: 0.75rem (12px); border-end-end-radius: 0.75rem (12px); |
| `rounded-e-2xl` | border-start-end-radius: 1rem (16px); border-end-end-radius: 1rem (16px); |
| `rounded-e-3xl` | border-start-end-radius: 1.5rem (24px); border-end-end-radius: 1.5rem (24px); |
| `rounded-e-full` | border-start-end-radius: 9999px; border-end-end-radius: 9999px; |

### Top Side (rounded-t-*)

| Class | CSS Properties |
|-------|----------------|
| `rounded-t-none` | border-top-left-radius: 0px; border-top-right-radius: 0px; |
| `rounded-t-sm` | border-top-left-radius: 0.125rem (2px); border-top-right-radius: 0.125rem (2px); |
| `rounded-t` | border-top-left-radius: 0.25rem (4px); border-top-right-radius: 0.25rem (4px); |
| `rounded-t-md` | border-top-left-radius: 0.375rem (6px); border-top-right-radius: 0.375rem (6px); |
| `rounded-t-lg` | border-top-left-radius: 0.5rem (8px); border-top-right-radius: 0.5rem (8px); |
| `rounded-t-xl` | border-top-left-radius: 0.75rem (12px); border-top-right-radius: 0.75rem (12px); |
| `rounded-t-2xl` | border-top-left-radius: 1rem (16px); border-top-right-radius: 1rem (16px); |
| `rounded-t-3xl` | border-top-left-radius: 1.5rem (24px); border-top-right-radius: 1.5rem (24px); |
| `rounded-t-full` | border-top-left-radius: 9999px; border-top-right-radius: 9999px; |

### Right Side (rounded-r-*)

| Class | CSS Properties |
|-------|----------------|
| `rounded-r-none` | border-top-right-radius: 0px; border-bottom-right-radius: 0px; |
| `rounded-r-sm` | border-top-right-radius: 0.125rem (2px); border-bottom-right-radius: 0.125rem (2px); |
| `rounded-r` | border-top-right-radius: 0.25rem (4px); border-bottom-right-radius: 0.25rem (4px); |
| `rounded-r-md` | border-top-right-radius: 0.375rem (6px); border-bottom-right-radius: 0.375rem (6px); |
| `rounded-r-lg` | border-top-right-radius: 0.5rem (8px); border-bottom-right-radius: 0.5rem (8px); |
| `rounded-r-xl` | border-top-right-radius: 0.75rem (12px); border-bottom-right-radius: 0.75rem (12px); |
| `rounded-r-2xl` | border-top-right-radius: 1rem (16px); border-bottom-right-radius: 1rem (16px); |
| `rounded-r-3xl` | border-top-right-radius: 1.5rem (24px); border-bottom-right-radius: 1.5rem (24px); |
| `rounded-r-full` | border-top-right-radius: 9999px; border-bottom-right-radius: 9999px; |

### Bottom Side (rounded-b-*)

| Class | CSS Properties |
|-------|----------------|
| `rounded-b-none` | border-bottom-right-radius: 0px; border-bottom-left-radius: 0px; |
| `rounded-b-sm` | border-bottom-right-radius: 0.125rem (2px); border-bottom-left-radius: 0.125rem (2px); |
| `rounded-b` | border-bottom-right-radius: 0.25rem (4px); border-bottom-left-radius: 0.25rem (4px); |
| `rounded-b-md` | border-bottom-right-radius: 0.375rem (6px); border-bottom-left-radius: 0.375rem (6px); |
| `rounded-b-lg` | border-bottom-right-radius: 0.5rem (8px); border-bottom-left-radius: 0.5rem (8px); |
| `rounded-b-xl` | border-bottom-right-radius: 0.75rem (12px); border-bottom-left-radius: 0.75rem (12px); |
| `rounded-b-2xl` | border-bottom-right-radius: 1rem (16px); border-bottom-left-radius: 1rem (16px); |
| `rounded-b-3xl` | border-bottom-right-radius: 1.5rem (24px); border-bottom-left-radius: 1.5rem (24px); |
| `rounded-b-full` | border-bottom-right-radius: 9999px; border-bottom-left-radius: 9999px; |

## Usage Notes

- **General classes** (`rounded-*`) apply border-radius to all four corners
- **Side-specific classes** apply border-radius to specific sides or corners
- **Size variants** range from `none` (0px) to `full` (9999px for circular elements)
- **Directional classes** use logical properties (start/end) for better RTL support
- All values are based on Tailwind CSS default spacing scale
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new React project

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/create-react-app

Use npx to initialize a new React application with Create React App and then navigate into its directory.

```bash
npx create-react-app my-projectcd my-project
```

--------------------------------

### Create SvelteKit project

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/sveltekit

Initialize a new SvelteKit project and navigate into the project directory.

```terminal
npx sv create my-project
cd my-project
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Skew Usage

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/skew

Demonstrates how to apply skew transformations to elements using skew-x-* and skew-y-* utility classes. Shows examples of different skew intensities applied to image elements.

```APIDOC
## Basic Skew Usage

### Skewing an Element

Use the `skew-x-*` and `skew-y-*` utilities to skew an element.

### HTML Example
```html
<img class="skew-y-0 ...">
<img class="skew-y-3 ...">
<img class="skew-y-6 ...">
<img class="skew-y-12 ...">
```

### Description
Apply skew transformations by adding the appropriate utility class to your HTML element. The examples above show increasing levels of vertical skew (0, 3, 6, and 12 degrees).
```

--------------------------------

### Applying Transition Delay Utilities in HTML

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/transition-delay

Use `delay-*` utilities to control the transition delay of elements. This example demonstrates different delay values for buttons.

```html
<button class="transition delay-150 duration-300 ease-in-out ...">Button A</button>
<button class="transition delay-300 duration-300 ease-in-out ...">Button B</button>
<button class="transition delay-700 duration-300 ease-in-out ...">Button C</button>
```

--------------------------------

### Align Grid Item to Start with justify-self-start in HTML

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/justify-self

Use `justify-self-start` to align a grid item to the start of its inline axis.

```html
<div class="grid justify-items-stretch ...">
  <!-- ... -->
  <div class="justify-self-start ...">02</div>
  <!-- ... -->
  <!-- ... -->
  <!-- ... -->
  <!-- ... -->
</div>
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new Astro project

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/astro

Use `create astro` to initialize a new Astro project and navigate into its directory.

```bash
npm create astro@latest my-projectcd my-project
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new Ruby on Rails project

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/ruby-on-rails

Use the Rails command line to initialize a new project and navigate into its directory.

```bash
rails new my-projectcd my-project
```

--------------------------------

### Create new Nuxt project

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/nuxtjs

Initialize a new Nuxt project using the CLI and navigate into the project directory.

```bash
npx nuxi init my-projectcd my-project
```

--------------------------------

### Install tailwindcss-rails gem and generate config

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/ruby-on-rails

Add the tailwindcss-rails gem to your project and run the install command to create the tailwind.config.js file.

```bash
./bin/bundle add tailwindcss-rails./bin/rails tailwindcss:install
```

--------------------------------

### Create new Phoenix project

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/phoenix

Initialize a new Phoenix project using the mix command.

```bash
mix phx.new myprojectcd myproject
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new Symfony project

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/symfony

Initialize a new Symfony web application using the Symfony CLI.

```terminal
symfony new --webapp my-projectcd my-project
```

--------------------------------

### CSS Specificity and Declaration Order Example

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/adding-custom-styles

Demonstrates how CSS declaration order determines which rule wins when selectors have equal specificity. Later rules override earlier ones.

```CSS
.btn {
  background: blue;
  /* ... */
}

.bg-black {
  background: black;
}
```

```HTML
<button class="btn bg-black">...</button>
<button class="bg-black btn">...</button>
```

--------------------------------

### Justify grid items to the start

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/justify-items

Use `justify-items-start` to align grid items to the start of their inline axis within a grid container.

```html
<div class="grid justify-items-start ...">
  <div>01</div>
  <div>02</div>
  <div>03</div>
  <div>04</div>
  <div>05</div>
  <div>06</div>
</div>
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Scale Utilities to Elements in HTML

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/scale

Use `scale-*`, `scale-x-*`, and `scale-y-*` classes to resize elements. These examples demonstrate scaling an image to 75%, 100%, and 125% of its original size.

```html
<img class="scale-75 ...">
<img class="scale-100 ...">
<img class="scale-125 ...">
```

--------------------------------

### Using Arbitrary Transition Delay Values in HTML

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/transition-delay

Apply one-off `transition-delay` values using arbitrary value syntax. This example sets a delay of 2000ms directly in the class attribute.

```html
<div class="delay-[2000ms]">
  <!-- ... -->
</div>
```

--------------------------------

### Applying Responsive Transition Delay with Breakpoints

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/transition-delay

Use responsive variant modifiers to apply transition delay utilities at specific screen sizes. This example applies a different delay for medium screens and above.

```html
<div class="transition duration-300 delay-150 md:delay-300">
  <!-- ... -->
</div>
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Min-Width Usage

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/min-width

Demonstrates how to set the minimum width of elements using min-w-* utility classes. Shows practical examples of applying different minimum width values to nested elements.

```APIDOC
## Setting the Minimum Width

### Description
Set the minimum width of an element using `min-w-*` utilities. This ensures that an element will not shrink below the specified minimum width, even if its container is smaller.

### Usage Example

```html
<div class="w-96 ...">
  <div class="min-w-80 ...">min-w-80</div>
  <div class="min-w-64 ...">min-w-64</div>
  <div class="min-w-48 ...">min-w-48</div>
  <div class="min-w-40 ...">min-w-40</div>
  <div class="min-w-32 ...">min-w-32</div>
  <div class="min-w-24 ...">min-w-24</div>
  <div class="min-w-full ...">min-w-full</div>
</div>
```

### Explanation
In this example, a parent container with width `w-96` (24rem/384px) contains multiple child elements, each with different minimum width constraints. The `min-w-*` classes prevent these elements from shrinking below their specified minimum widths.
```

--------------------------------

### Aligning an item to the start

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/align-self

Use self-start to align an item to the start of the container’s cross axis, despite the container’s align-items value.

```html
<div class="flex items-stretch ...">
  <div>01</div>
  <div class="self-start ...">02</div>
  <div>03</div>
</div>
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize with Full Default Configuration

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/configuration

CLI command to scaffold a complete configuration file including all of Tailwind's default settings using the --full flag.

```bash
npx tailwindcss init --full
```

--------------------------------

### Create new Gatsby project

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/gatsby

Initialize a new Gatsby project using the Gatsby CLI.

```bash
gatsby new my-projectcd my-project
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new Angular project

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/angular

Use the Angular CLI to initialize a new project and navigate into its directory.

```Terminal
ng new my-projectcd my-project
```

--------------------------------

### Set gradient starting color with from-* utility

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/gradient-color-stops

Use from-* utilities to define the starting color of a gradient. Combine with a gradient direction class like bg-gradient-to-r.

```HTML
<div class="bg-gradient-to-r from-indigo-500 ..."></div>
```

--------------------------------

### Create new Remix project

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/remix

Initialize a new Remix project using Create Remix. Run this command in your terminal to scaffold the project structure.

```bash
npx create-remix@latest my-projectcd my-project
```

--------------------------------

### Starting and ending grid lines with col-start and col-end

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/grid-column

Use col-start-* and col-end-* utilities to position elements at specific grid lines. These can be combined with col-span-* to control both position and span. CSS grid lines start at 1, not 0.

```HTML
<div class="grid grid-cols-6 gap-4">
  <div class="col-start-2 col-span-4 ...">01</div>
  <div class="col-start-1 col-end-3 ...">02</div>
  <div class="col-end-7 col-span-2 ...">03</div>
  <div class="col-start-1 col-end-7 ...">04</div>
</div>
```

--------------------------------

### Starting and ending grid lines with row-start and row-end

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/grid-row

Use row-start-* and row-end-* utilities to position elements at specific grid lines. CSS grid lines start at 1, not 0; combine with row-span-* for precise control over row placement.

```HTML
<div class="grid grid-rows-3 grid-flow-col gap-4">
  <div class="row-start-2 row-span-2 ...">01</div>
  <div class="row-end-3 row-span-2 ...">02</div>
  <div class="row-start-1 row-end-4 ...">03</div>
</div>
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize with PostCSS Configuration

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/configuration

CLI command to generate both tailwind.config.js and postcss.config.js files in one step using the -p flag.

```bash
npx tailwindcss init -p
```

--------------------------------

### GET /border-y-{color}

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/border-color

Sets the border color for the top and bottom sides of an element.

```APIDOC
## GET /border-y-{color}

### Description
Sets the border color for the top and bottom sides of an element using Tailwind CSS utility classes.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/border-y-{color}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **color** (string) - Required - The color name and shade (e.g., slate-500, zinc-400, gray-50).

### Request Example
{
  "class": "border-y-slate-500"
}

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **border-top-color** (string) - CSS color value
- **border-bottom-color** (string) - CSS color value

#### Response Example
{
  "border-top-color": "rgb(100 116 139 / var(--tw-border-opacity, 1))",
  "border-bottom-color": "rgb(100 116 139 / var(--tw-border-opacity, 1))"
}
```

--------------------------------

### GET /border-x-{color}

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/border-color

Sets the border color for the left and right sides of an element.

```APIDOC
## GET /border-x-{color}

### Description
Sets the border color for the left and right sides of an element using Tailwind CSS utility classes.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/border-x-{color}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **color** (string) - Required - The color name and shade (e.g., rose-950).

### Request Example
{
  "class": "border-x-rose-950"
}

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **border-left-color** (string) - CSS color value
- **border-right-color** (string) - CSS color value

#### Response Example
{
  "border-left-color": "rgb(76 5 25 / var(--tw-border-opacity, 1))",
  "border-right-color": "rgb(76 5 25 / var(--tw-border-opacity, 1))"
}
```

--------------------------------

### GET .max-h-[{value}]

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/max-height

Generates a max-height property on the fly using an arbitrary value.

```APIDOC
## GET .max-h-[{value}]

### Description
Use square brackets to generate a property using any arbitrary CSS value that isn't part of the theme.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
.max-h-[{value}]

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **value** (string) - Required - Any valid CSS length value (e.g., 220px, 50vh, 15rem).

### Request Example
<div class="max-h-[220px]">
  <!-- Content -->
</div>

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **max-height** (string) - The custom CSS value applied.

#### Response Example
{
  "max-height": "220px"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Create SolidJS project

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/solidjs

Initialize a new SolidJS project using the Vite template via degit.

```terminal
npx degit solidjs/templates/js my-projectcd my-project
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new AdonisJS project

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/adonisjs

Use `npm init adonisjs` to scaffold a new AdonisJS web project.

```bash
npm init adonisjs@latest my-project -- --kit=webcd my-project
```

--------------------------------

### Apply a local preset with project-specific extensions

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/presets

Import a local preset file and add project-specific theme extensions. Project configuration merges with the preset, with project values taking precedence for top-level theme keys.

```javascript
/** @type {import('tailwindcss').Config} */
module.exports = {
  presets: [
    require('./my-preset.js')
  ],
  // Customizations specific to this project would go here
  theme: {
    extend: {
      minHeight: {
        48: '12rem',
      }
    }
  },
}
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Responsive Utility Prefixes

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/responsive-design

Prefix any utility class with a breakpoint name to apply it conditionally at different screen sizes.

```html
<!-- Width of 16 by default, 32 on medium screens, and 48 on large screens -->
<img class="w-16 md:w-32 lg:w-48" src="...">
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize with Custom Configuration File Name

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/configuration

CLI command to generate a Tailwind config file with a custom name instead of the default tailwind.config.js.

```bash
npx tailwindcss init tailwindcss-config.js
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new Rspack project

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/rspack

Use the Rspack CLI to initialize a new project.

```Terminal
npm create rspack@latest
```

--------------------------------

### CLASS .row-start-{n} / .row-end-{n}

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/grid-row

Utilities for positioning an element at specific starting or ending grid lines.

```APIDOC
## CLASS .row-start-{n} / .row-end-{n}

### Description
Use the row-start-* and row-end-* utilities to make an element start or end at the nth grid line. These can be combined with row-span utilities.

### Method
CLASS

### Endpoint
.row-start-{n} / .row-end-{n}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **n** (string|number) - Required - The grid line index (1-13) or 'auto'. Note that CSS grid lines start at 1.

### Request Example
<div class="grid grid-rows-3 grid-flow-col gap-4">
  <div class="row-start-2 row-span-2">01</div>
  <div class="row-end-3 row-span-2">02</div>
  <div class="row-start-1 row-end-4">03</div>
</div>

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **grid-row-start** (string) - The CSS property for the starting line.
- **grid-row-end** (string) - The CSS property for the ending line.

#### Response Example
{
  "grid-row-start": "2",
  "grid-row-end": "auto"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Implement Mobile-First Styling

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/responsive-design

Use unprefixed utilities for mobile and prefixed utilities to override them for larger screens.

```html
<!-- This will only center text on screens 640px and wider, not on small screens -->
<div class="sm:text-center"></div>
```

```html
<!-- This will center text on mobile, and left align it on screens 640px and wider -->
<div class="text-center sm:text-left"></div>
```

--------------------------------

### GET .max-h-{size}

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/max-height

Sets the maximum height of an element using the default spacing scale or specific keywords.

```APIDOC
## GET .max-h-{size}

### Description
Utility classes for setting the maximum height of an element. The scale includes values from 0 to 96, as well as percentage and viewport-based units.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
.max-h-{size}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **size** (string|number) - Required - The size key from the spacing scale (e.g., 0, 0.5, 1, 4, 96) or keywords (none, full, screen, svh, lvh, dvh, min, max, fit).

### Request Example
<div class="h-96 ...">
  <div class="h-full max-h-80 ...">max-h-80</div>
</div>

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **max-height** (string) - The resulting CSS max-height property value.

#### Response Example
{
  "max-height": "20rem" /* 320px */
}
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Important Modifier to Utilities

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/configuration

Add a ! character to the start of a utility to force !important. Place the ! after variants but before any custom prefixes.

```html
<p class="!font-medium font-bold">
  This will be medium even though bold comes later in the CSS.
</p>
```

```html
<div class="sm:hover:!tw-font-bold">
```

--------------------------------

### Use Complete Class Names Dynamically (HTML)

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/content-configuration

Demonstrates the correct way to apply dynamic classes in HTML by ensuring that full class names are present as static strings.

```html
<div class="{{ error ? 'text-red-600' : 'text-green-600' }}"></div>
```

--------------------------------

### GET /vertical-align/utilities

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/vertical-align

Retrieve and apply standard vertical alignment utility classes to control the positioning of inline or table-cell boxes.

```APIDOC
## GET /vertical-align/utilities

### Description
Utilities for controlling the vertical alignment of an inline or table-cell box.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/vertical-align/utilities

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **align-baseline** (string) - Required - vertical-align: baseline;
- **align-top** (string) - Required - vertical-align: top;
- **align-middle** (string) - Required - vertical-align: middle;
- **align-bottom** (string) - Required - vertical-align: bottom;
- **align-text-top** (string) - Required - vertical-align: text-top;
- **align-text-bottom** (string) - Required - vertical-align: text-bottom;
- **align-sub** (string) - Required - vertical-align: sub;
- **align-super** (string) - Required - vertical-align: super;

### Request Example
<span class="inline-block align-baseline">...</span>

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **css_property** (string) - The vertical-align CSS property applied to the element.

#### Response Example
{
  "vertical-align": "baseline"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Responsive Min-Width with Breakpoints

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/min-width

Explains how to use responsive breakpoint modifiers to apply different min-width values at different screen sizes. Demonstrates using the md: variant for medium and larger screens.

```APIDOC
## Breakpoints and Media Queries

### Description
You can use variant modifiers to target media queries like responsive breakpoints, dark mode, prefers-reduced-motion, and more. This allows you to apply different min-width values based on screen size and other media query conditions.

### Available Responsive Modifiers
Common breakpoint modifiers include: `sm:`, `md:`, `lg:`, `xl:`, `2xl:`, and others for responsive design.

### Usage Example

```html
<div class="w-24 min-w-full md:min-w-0">
  <!-- ... -->
</div>
```

### Explanation
In this example:
- On small screens, the element has a minimum width of `min-w-full` (100%)
- On medium screens and above (md breakpoint), the minimum width changes to `md:min-w-0` (0px)

Other media query modifiers available:
- Dark mode: `dark:min-w-*`
- Prefers reduced motion: `motion-safe:min-w-*`, `motion-reduce:min-w-*`
- Print styles: `print:min-w-*`

For more information, refer to the Responsive Design and Dark Mode documentation.
```

--------------------------------

### GET .{variant}:max-h-{size}

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/max-height

Applies max-height utilities conditionally based on states like hover or responsive breakpoints.

```APIDOC
## GET .{variant}:max-h-{size}

### Description
Tailwind allows you to apply utility classes in different states using variant modifiers for responsive design and pseudo-classes.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
.{variant}:max-h-{size}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **variant** (string) - Required - The modifier state (e.g., hover, focus) or breakpoint (e.g., sm, md, lg, xl).
- **size** (string) - Required - The max-height size value.

### Request Example
<div class="h-48 max-h-full hover:max-h-screen md:max-h-screen">
  <!-- Content -->
</div>

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **css_rule** (string) - The generated CSS rule with the appropriate media query or pseudo-selector.

#### Response Example
{
  "@media (min-width: 768px)": {
    ".md:max-h-screen": {
      "max-height": "100vh"
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Grid with place-items-start alignment

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/place-items

Aligns grid items to the start of their grid areas on both axes. Useful for left-aligned or top-aligned grid layouts.

```HTML
<div class="grid grid-cols-3 gap-4 place-items-start ...">
  <div>01</div>
  <div>02</div>
  <div>03</div>
  <div>04</div>
  <div>05</div>
  <div>06</div>
</div>
```

--------------------------------

### Create Parcel project structure

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/parcel

Initialize a new Parcel project with npm and create the src directory structure.

```bash
mkdir my-projectcd my-projectnpm init -ynpm install -D parcelmkdir srctouch src/index.html
```

--------------------------------

### Example of unintended class detection in HTML

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/content-configuration

Demonstrates how plain text content can lead to the accidental generation of classes like 'container'.

```html
<div class="text-lg leading-8 text-gray-600">
  Every custom pool we design starts as a used shipping container, and is
  retrofitted with state of the art technology and finishes to turn it into
  a beautiful and functional way to entertain your guests all summer long.
</div>
```

--------------------------------

### Create Next.js project with TypeScript

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/nextjs

Initialize a new Next.js project with TypeScript and ESLint enabled using Create Next App.

```bash
npx create-next-app@latest my-project --typescript --eslintcd my-project
```

--------------------------------

### JavaScript Class Toggling Example

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/content-configuration

Demonstrates using JavaScript to toggle Tailwind classes, such as 'hidden' and 'block', for dynamic UI changes.

```javascript
<script>
  menuButton.addEventListener('click', function () {
    let classList = document.getElementById('nav').classList
    classList.toggle('hidden')
    classList.toggle('block')
  })
</script>
```

--------------------------------

### Install Official Plugins in tailwind.config.js

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/plugins

Add official Tailwind plugins (typography, forms, aspect-ratio, container-queries) to your configuration by requiring them in the plugins array.

```JavaScript
/** @type {import('tailwindcss').Config} */
module.exports = {
  // ...
  plugins: [
    require('@tailwindcss/typography'),
    require('@tailwindcss/forms'),
    require('@tailwindcss/aspect-ratio'),
    require('@tailwindcss/container-queries'),
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Style first and last children in Tailwind CSS

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/hover-focus-and-other-states

Use first:pt-0 and last:pb-0 to remove padding from the start and end of a list.

```html
<ul role="list" class="p-6 divide-y divide-slate-200">
  {#each people as person}
    <!-- Remove top/bottom padding when first/last child -->
    <li class="flex py-4 first:pt-0 last:pb-0">
      <img class="h-10 w-10 rounded-full" src="{person.imageUrl}" alt="" />
      <div class="ml-3 overflow-hidden">
        <p class="text-sm font-medium text-slate-900">{person.name}</p>
        <p class="text-sm text-slate-500 truncate">{person.email}</p>
      </div>
    </li>
  {/each}
</ul>
```

--------------------------------

### Use @config directive (incorrect ordering)

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/functions-and-directives

This example shows the incorrect pattern of placing @config before @import statements, which will not work correctly with postcss-import.

```CSS
@config "./tailwind.admin.config.js";

@import "tailwindcss/base";
@import "./custom-base.css";
@import "tailwindcss/components";
@import "./custom-components.css";
@import "tailwindcss/utilities";
```

--------------------------------

### Create Laravel project via Composer

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/laravel

Initializes a new Laravel application using the Composer create-project command.

```terminal
composer create-project laravel/laravel my-projectcd my-project
```

--------------------------------

### Configure template paths

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/guides/qwik

Update tailwind.config.js to include paths to all Qwik component and template files.

```javascript
/** @type {import('tailwindcss').Config} */
module.exports = {
  content: ['./src/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx,mdx}'],
  theme: {
    extend: {},
  },
  plugins: [],
}

```

--------------------------------

### Generated CSS Output with Important Flag

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/configuration

Example of Tailwind utilities generated with !important when the important option is enabled. All utilities include the !important declaration.

```css
.leading-none {
  line-height: 1 !important;
}
.leading-tight {
  line-height: 1.25 !important;
}
.leading-snug {
  line-height: 1.375 !important;
}
/* etc. */
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Tailwind Configuration

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/configuration

CLI command to generate a minimal tailwind.config.js file at the project root using the Tailwind CLI utility.

```bash
npx tailwindcss init
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Prefix to Negative Values

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/configuration

Negative utility values place the dash before the custom prefix. For example, with tw- prefix, -mt-8 becomes -tw-mt-8.

```html
<div class="-tw-mt-8">
  <!-- -->
</div>
```

--------------------------------

### Create configurable plugin with plugin.withOptions

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/plugins

Use plugin.withOptions to allow users to pass custom options when registering the plugin, such as custom class names or other configuration that doesn't belong in theme.

```javascript
const plugin = require('tailwindcss/plugin')

module.exports = plugin.withOptions(function (options = {}) {
  return function({ addComponents }) {
    const className = options.className ?? 'markdown'

    addComponents({
      [`.${className}`]: {
        // ...
      }
    })
  }
}, function (options) {
  return {
    theme: {
      markdown: {
        // ...
      }
    },
  }
})
```

--------------------------------

### Update Tailwind CSS and dependencies via npm

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/upgrade-guide

Install the latest versions of Tailwind CSS, PostCSS, and Autoprefixer. Note that v3.0 requires PostCSS 8.

```shell
npm install -D tailwindcss@latest postcss@latest autoprefixer@latest
```

```shell
npm install -D tailwindcss@latest \
  @tailwindcss/typography@latest \
  @tailwindcss/forms@latest \
  @tailwindcss/aspect-ratio@latest \
  @tailwindcss/line-clamp@latest \
  postcss@latest \
  autoprefixer@latest
```

--------------------------------

### Configure prefix and important options

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/plugins

Plugin utilities automatically respect global configuration settings for prefixes and importance.

```javascript
/** @type {import('tailwindcss').Config} */
module.exports = {
  prefix: 'tw-',
  important: true,
  // ...
}
```

--------------------------------

### Use postcss-nesting with tailwindcss/nesting

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/using-with-preprocessors

Install postcss-nesting separately and pass it as an argument to tailwindcss/nesting to use the standard CSS Nesting specification instead of the default Sass-like syntax.

```JavaScript
// postcss.config.js
module.exports = {
  plugins: {
    'postcss-import': {},
    'tailwindcss/nesting': 'postcss-nesting',
    tailwindcss: {},
    autoprefixer: {},
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Add custom col-start-* utilities in Tailwind config

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/grid-column

Extend the gridColumnStart section to add new col-start-* utilities for specifying grid column start lines.

```javascript
module.exports = {
  theme: {
    extend: {
      gridColumnStart: {
        '13': '13',
        '14': '14',
        '15': '15',
        '16': '16',
        '17': '17',
      }
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Using Arbitrary Drop Shadow Values

Source: https://v3.tailwindcss.com/docs/drop-shadow

Demonstrates how to use arbitrary drop shadow values with square bracket notation for one-off values that don't need to be added to the theme configuration.

```APIDOC
## Using Arbitrary Drop Shadow Values

### Description
Use square bracket notation to generate drop shadow utilities on the fly with arbitrary values that don't need to be added to your theme configuration.

### Usage

```html
<div class="drop-shadow-[0_35px_35px_rgba(0,0,0,0.25)]">
  <!-- Element with custom drop shadow value -->
</div>
```

### Syntax
- Use square brackets: drop-shadow-[value]
- Replace spaces with underscores in the value
- Supports any valid CSS drop-shadow filter value

### Notes
For more information about arbitrary value support, refer to the arbitrary values documentation.
```
